extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is a 12-way conjunction that INCLUDES pregnancy as a conjunct. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), whose antecedent is simply pregnancy. Because the conjunction for rec_drug_a entails pregnancy, any input that fires the rec_drug_a rule necessarily also satisfies the rec_drug_b trigger. The two rules target different outputs, so priority/tie-breaking (which only applies per-output) does not suppress either. Setting all 12 conjuncts (pregnancy, g6pd_deficiency, polypharmacy, immunosuppressed, prior_gi_bleed, qt_prolongation, peptic_ulcer, ckd_dialysis, renal_impairment, elderly, asthma, anticoagulated) to true makes rec_drug_a := true and, via pregnancy=true, rec_drug_b := true simultaneously. seizure_history and hypertension only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set false. Thus rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property.
